author: takeshy
description: A modern WYSIWYG Markdown editor. Edit your notes with a rich text interface while keeping pure Markdown. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: takeshy
manifest.description: Edit your Markdown notes with a modern WYSIWYG rich text interface while keeping pure Markdown.
manifest.name: Wysimark Editor

name: Wysimark Editor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
✨ New: Inline embeds

 Obsidian embeds (![[note]], ![[image.png]], ![[doc.pdf]]) now render inline inside the WYSIWYG editor instead of showing up as a  broken image.

  - Embedded notes, images, and other files are rendered live using Obsidian's own renderer, resolved relative to the current file.
  - Select an embed to reveal a small toolbar to edit or remove it.
  - The link edit dialog gains an "Insert as embed" checkbox, so you can toggle any internal link [[…]] into an embed ![[…]] (and  back).
  - Full Markdown round-trip is preserved — embeds serialize back to standard ![[…]] syntax.

 🐛 Fixes & internals (from v1.1.1)

  - Fixed a memory leak: internal link previews now use a short-lived render lifecycle owner instead of the long-lived plugin  instance.
  - Resolved lint warnings (unnecessary regex escapes, control-character regex, unused imports/variables).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved
  - Improved the internal link popup in Wysimark:
    - Internal links now open the linked Obsidian note correctly.
    - The popup now shows the internal link path, action buttons, and preview in a single-column layout.
    - Link previews are rendered using Obsidian's Markdown renderer.
    - Long URLs and preview content wrap inside the popup instead of overflowing.

  ### Changed
  - Removed the current-line background highlight while editing code blocks, leaving only the text cursor visible for a cleaner
  editing experience.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Add Obsidian wiki link support

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fiexed
Fixed an issue where block quotes separated by a blank line could be automatically merged in the editor.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Changed
  - Adjusted spacing for paragraphs, headings, blockquotes, lists, and code blocks in the Wysimark editor.
  - Improved the editor layout to better match Obsidian’s rendered Markdown view.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - Fixed GFM task list parsing so checkbox items like `- [x]` and `- [ ]` are correctly handled as task list items.
  - Added round-trip coverage to ensure task list items serialize back to Markdown checkbox syntax correctly.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Replace dynamic <script> creation in React DOM during bundle and bump package version to 1.0.37 by @takeshy in https://github.com/takeshy/obsidian-wysimark/pull/6


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/takeshy/obsidian-wysimark/compare/1.0.36...1.0.37

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - Reduced unnecessary backslash escaping when opening and saving Markdown without edits.
  - Preserved plain text such as pipes, brackets, asterisks, lone backticks, underscores, tildes, comparison symbols,
  and Windows-style paths more accurately during Markdown round-trip.
  - Fixed escaping for pipes inside table cells so tables with literal `|` characters round-trip correctly.
  - Fixed link label serialization so brackets inside link text do not break Markdown links.
  - Fixed inline `<mark>...</mark>` round-trip by parsing and serializing it as highlighted text.

  ### Internal
  - Ported recent Wysimark escape / highlight round-trip fixes.
  - Added round-trip tests for escape behavior, tables, anchors, and highlight markup.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - Fixed Obsidian plugin scanner errors by updating the minimum supported Obsidian version to `1.7.2`.
  - Fixed popout window compatibility warnings by using `window.setTimeout()`, `window.clearTimeout()`, and  `activeDocument` where required.
  - Fixed cross-window input event checking by replacing `instanceof InputEvent` with `.instanceOf(InputEvent)`.

### Changed
  - Replaced `lodash.throttle` with a local throttle implementation.
  - Removed unused or discouraged dependencies: `builtin-modules`, `lodash.debounce`, and `lodash.throttle`.
  - Added `mdast-util-gfm-table` as a direct dependency.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Changed
  - Updated the release workflow to generate GitHub artifact attestations for release assets.

### Fixed
  - Fixed release provenance verification errors for `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` by generating
  attestations during the GitHub Actions release job.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Changed

  - Updated the release workflow to create Obsidian-compatible release tags without a `v` prefix.
    - Release tags now match the exact version in `manifest.json`, for example `1.0.34`.
    - Draft release workflow is unchanged, so release notes can still be edited manually before publishing.
  - Updated the version bump script to avoid adding unnecessary `versions.json` entries when the required Obsidian
  version has not changed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.33

  - Addressed Obsidian review lint findings.
  - Replaced deprecated React `MutableRefObject` usage with `RefObject`.
  - Updated a non-ASCII detection regular expression to avoid control-character lint warnings.
releases.name: v1.0.33

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed

 - Fixed handling of non-ASCII `insertText` input events in the editor.
 - Improved text insertion behavior for Japanese and other IME/non-ASCII input paths where browser input events could  bypass Slate's normal text insertion flow.
releases.name: v1.0.32

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.31

  - Remove unnecessary escape of `[` inside a regex character class in `escapeText` (flagged by the Obsidian plugin  review bot). No behavioral change.
releases.name: v1.0.31

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Improvements ### Cleaner Markdown on save — fewer spurious backslashes Inline escaping is now context-aware, so text that doesn't actually need escaping round-trips cleanly: - Windows paths — C:\Users\name no longer becomes C:\\Users\\name. \ is only escaped when followed by ASCII punctuation. - Less-than in expressions — a < b and a<5 && 5>b stay literal. < is only escaped when it could start an HTML tag or autolink (
, , ). - Identifiers and filenames — foo_bar, my_var_name, python_3 no longer get \_ injected. _ and ~ are only escaped when they could open/close emphasis/strikethrough (not between word-like characters, per CommonMark flanking rules). - Unicode identifiers also preserved (変数_名, あ_い). ### Internal:
in table cells Restored the single-table-content-per-cell invariant in the serializer that had regressed. Round-trip (
↔ embedded \n) now preserved under a stable structure; added round-trip tests.
releases.name: v1.0.30

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Improved line break support in table cells Improved round-trip handling of soft line breaks (Shift+Enter) within table cells. ### Changes - Table cell line breaks are now serialized as
tags in Markdown output - \ /\
/ \
tags are now parsed as soft line breaks on import - Multi-line content in table cells is now correctly preserved across save and reload ### Fixes - Fixed GFM table rows breaking when cells contain line breaks - Fixed multi-line content in table cells being lost after save
releases.name: v1.0.29

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Internal
- Fixed lint errors in test file
releases.name: v1.0.28

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features - Soft break support with Shift+Enter - Shift+Enter now inserts a line break (
) within the same paragraph - Enter continues to create a new paragraph as before - Serialized as trailing two-space + newline ( \n) in Markdown ## Bug Fixes - Blank lines no longer lost on save/reload - Blank lines between paragraphs are now preserved through the Markdown round-trip - Recovers consecutive blank lines collapsed by remark-parse using MDAST node position gaps - Fixed trim() stripping NBSP blank-line markers by switching to ASCII-only whitespace trimming - Empty paragraphs no longer collapse to zero height in the editor - Added min-height: 1em to non-collapsible empty paragraphs so they remain visible and clickable
releases.name: v1.0.27

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix Enter key not working on mobile after IME confirmation
releases.name: v1.0.26

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes
- Code block: Fix off-by-one error in syntax highlight decoration
- Table: Prevent crash when selection range is empty or element path is stale
- Anchor dialog: Prevent dialog from overflowing outside the viewport
- Serialization: Preserve empty paragraphs when converting to Markdown (using NBSP)
- Serialization: Return empty array instead of throwing when anchor has no text segments
- Autocomplete marks: Correctly handle multi-byte Unicode characters (e.g. emoji) in inline formatting
- Task list: Return true from toggleTaskListItem to indicate handled action

## Improvements
- Toolbar: Replace window.resize event + debounce with ResizeObserver for more reliable responsive toolbar
- Toolbar: Use Slate's Path.equals() instead of JSON.stringify for path comparison
- Anchor dialog: Use flexible width (max-width) instead of fixed width for better responsiveness
- Panel overlay: Set explicit text color and max-width to prevent overflow
- Layout: Fix flexbox sizing for editor and outer container
- React hooks: Fix missing dependencies in useCallback for tooltip and toolbar button
- Block quote: Remove redundant truthiness checks
releases.name: v1.0.25

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New

### Editing Improvements
  - **Backspace outdent/unlist**: Pressing Backspace at the beginning of a list item now outdents it (reduces depth), or converts it
  to a paragraph at depth 0 — matching the behavior of Google Docs and Notio
releases.name: v1.0.24

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Bug Fixes

  - Fix toolbar resize handler by switching from throttle to debounce for reliable
   icon switching on window resize
releases.name: v1.0.23

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Improvements
- Use relative font size for better accessibility: Changed font-size from hardcoded 16px to 1rem in the editor and tooltips, allowing the editor to respect Obsidian's font size settings
releases.name: v1.0.22

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes
  - Replace navigator.userAgent usage with Obsidian's Platform API for OS detection
  - Remove unnecessary Firefox-specific workaround (Obsidian runs on Electron/Chromium)
  - Fix deprecated React.FormEvent type in image upload dialog
releases.name: v1.0.21

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Bug Fixes

  - Markdown escaping: Fix over-escaping of ASCII punctuation (., ,, !, ?, : etc.) in saved Markdown. Only characters
  that genuinely need escaping are now escaped.
  - Blockquote serialization: Fix blockquote losing indentation due to incorrect .trim().
  - Image resize: Fix height/width comparison bug in resizeInBounds and add event listener cleanup on unmount.
  - Table: Fix cell join() missing separator and column insertion boundary access at end.
  - Marks: Fix wrong variable in merge-adjacent-spaces (node.code -> nextNode.code).
  - URL parsing: Fix parseUrl regex to support relative URLs without leading slash; fix unescapeUrlSlashes to only
  unescape forward slashes.
  - Placeholder safety: Use BEL character placeholders to avoid conflicts with user content.
  - Code block: Fix loose equality (==) to strict (===).
  - Misc: Add radix parameter to all parseInt calls, fix unsafe ref cast in image-url-dialog, stabilize throttle/refresh
   hooks to prevent unnecessary recreations.

  Improvements

  - Atomic file saving: Use Vault.process() instead of Vault.modify() for safer, race-condition-free saving.
  - File loading performance: Use vault.cachedRead() for normal file loading (explicit reload still reads from disk).
  - Non-intrusive sidebar: The editor panel is no longer auto-created on plugin load. Use the ribbon icon or "Toggle
  sidebar" command to open it.
  - Plugin lifecycle: Add explicit onunload() method.
  - Remove unused @portive/client dependency
releases.name: v1.0.20

releases.author: takeshy
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes

- Fixed URL escaping in HTML blocks: URLs inside HTML blocks (such as <iframe>, <video>, embedded content) are no longer incorrectly escaped. This fixes issues where embedded content URLs were being modified and becoming invalid.
- Fixed view lifecycle management: Added proper super.onClose() call to ensure correct cleanup when the Wysimark view is closed. This improves stability and compatibility with Obsidian's plugin lifecycle.
releases.name: v1.0.19

releases.author: takeshy
releases.description:
## What's New

  ### Link Dialog Improvements
  - **Text and Tooltip fields**: Link insert dialog now includes text and tooltip input fields
  - **Selected text support**: When text is selected, it automatically becomes the link text
  - **Sync feature**: Tooltip automatically syncs with link text until manually edited
  - **Edit support**: Existing links can now have their text edited (not just URL and tooltip)

  ### Table Navigation Enhancement
  - **Enter key**: Now inserts line break within cell (soft break)
  - **Tab key**: Moves to next cell; exits table at last cell
  - **Shift+Enter**: Moves to next cell; adds new row at last cell
  - **Shift+Tab**: Moves to previous cell

  ### Bug Fixes
  - Fixed "Cannot get leaf node" error when converting to heading with shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+1-6)
  - Reduced paragraph margins for tighter spacing
releases.name: Link Dialog Enhancement & Table Navigation Improvement
